findings.design_reference: FR-020 / NFR-001 / specs/198-beta2-hardening/data-model.md:99-114 / architecture-core, integration-api, code-implementation and test-integrity lenses
findings.comment: HUMAN DECISION REQUIRED: stop the autonomous review/fix loop. The prior blocking finding remains unresolved and no worktree-visible human deferral covers it. The new code canonicalizes status enums and rejects injected capacity/test-count lines, but the authoritative TrackerClaims model says the deterministic state.md + tasks-progress.yml parse includes capacity and test_counts and subset-compares every claim against accepted review verdict + run records. Lines 74-78 instead categorically reject those claims without parsing or comparison, and Test 10 only proves rejection. This does not implement the prior requested complete canonical parser or prove legitimate capacity/test-count reconciliation. A human must decide whether to require that contract implementation plus paired legitimate/abuse tests, or record an explicit visible deferral naming the issue, approver, and carried work.

findings.design_reference: FR-020 / NFR-001 / specs/198-beta2-hardening/data-model.md:99-114 / code-implementation and test-integrity lenses
findings.comment: HUMAN DECISION REQUIRED: stop the autonomous review/fix loop. The prior blocking parser finding remains partially unresolved and no worktree-visible human deferral covers it. Although canonical status enums and injected capacity are now rejected, Last Completed Task still uses prefix matches (`^T\d{3}\b` and `^\(none`) and therefore accepts noncanonical claims such as `T001 everything shipped` or `(none) but complete`; when T001 has a pass verdict, the former can receive the tracker-only freshness bypass. Test 9 only checks free text that does not begin with a task id, so it does not falsify this surviving path. A human must require exact whole-field parsing plus paired abuse tests, or record a visible approved deferral naming the issue and carried work.
